title: Why Libraries Matter More Than Ever
subtitle: 数字时代，图书馆为何反而更重要了

paragraphs.en: Librarians know the value of their community services, and their patrons appreciate their importance as well. But in an increasingly digital world, we see the role of libraries as community and cultural centers at times undervalued. When shrinking municipal budgets combine with the nonstop technological revolution, public library services can seem old-fashioned.
paragraphs.zh: 图书馆员深知其社区服务的价值，读者也同样珍视其重要性。但在日益数字化的世界中，图书馆作为社区和文化中心的作用有时被低估。当不断缩减的市政预算与持续的技术革命交织，公共图书馆的服务可能显得过时。

paragraphs.en: But it is precisely those shrinking budgets and the assault of technologically mediated life that make public libraries' cultural and community offerings more important than ever. Many people point out the value public libraries bring to their communities. More than just books and banks of computers, libraries are still places where individuals gather to explore, interact, and imagine.
paragraphs.zh: 但正是预算的缩减和技术媒介生活的冲击，使得公共图书馆的文化和社区服务比以往更加重要。许多人指出公共图书馆为其社区带来的价值。图书馆不仅仅是书籍和电脑库，它仍是人们聚集探索、互动和想象的地方。

paragraphs.en: Some of the specific ways in which libraries add value to our communities and serve as cultural centers are community builders, centers for the arts, and champions of youth. Libraries serve in these capacities and are more than just about information. As community builders, libraries are engaged in incredible work.
paragraphs.zh: 图书馆为社区增值并作为文化中心的一些具体方式是：社区建设者、艺术中心和青年守护者。图书馆在这些方面发挥作用，其意义远超信息本身。作为社区建设者，图书馆从事着非凡的工作。

paragraphs.en: From tiny public libraries to huge city institutions, libraries across the United States are building community and supporting local culture in exciting ways. These are inspiring and hopefully will encourage librarians interested in community services to make connections, share ideas, and build partnerships. Strengthening neighborhoods and championing the cultural lives of communities are big responsibilities.
paragraphs.zh: 从小型公共图书馆到庞大的城市机构，美国各地的图书馆正以激动人心的方式建设社区和支持本地文化。这些令人鼓舞，希望能激励对社区服务感兴趣的图书馆员相互联系、分享想法并建立伙伴关系。加强社区建设和倡导社区文化生活是重大责任。

paragraphs.en: Place-based economic development stresses the importance of offering attractive, functional, and community-based places, such as libraries, in town squares and depressed neighborhoods. Like a major department store in a mall, libraries attract large numbers of people, creating economic opportunities for numerous businesses in the surrounding area.
paragraphs.zh: 基于场所的经济发展强调在城镇广场和萧条社区提供有吸引力、功能齐全且以社区为基础的场所的重要性，如图书馆。就像购物中心的主力百货店一样，图书馆吸引大量人流，为周边众多企业创造经济机会。

paragraphs.en: Large cities, medium-sized ones, and even small towns have successfully transformed their libraries into the hubs of vibrant neighborhoods. As key municipal agencies, libraries are major players in creating livable, environmentally friendly cities and towns. The Urban Libraries Council released a report detailing the unique ways libraries can further sustainability at the local level.
paragraphs.zh: 大城市、中型城市甚至小城镇都已成功将图书馆转变为充满活力的社区枢纽。作为重要的市政机构，图书馆是创建宜居环保城镇的主要力量。城市图书馆理事会发布了一份报告，详述了图书馆在地方层面促进可持续发展的独特方式。

paragraphs.en: Beyond ensuring library construction projects consider environmental impact, libraries can lead in supporting local foods and craftsmen, like partnering with local businesses to pioneer a farmers' market or a seed lending library that fosters community resilience and a culture of sharing. Archives preserve historic artifacts, oral histories, and scholarly writings relevant to the community, including minority groups.
paragraphs.zh: 除了确保图书馆建设项目考虑环境影响外，图书馆还可以带头支持本地食品和工匠，比如与当地企业合作开创农贸市场，或设立培育社区韧性和分享文化的种子借阅图书馆。档案馆保存与社区相关的历史文物、口述历史和学术著作，包括少数群体。

paragraphs.en: Communities lucky enough to have archivists have a great advantage when it comes to organizing historical records. An organized archive is a place where people can research their ancestry, do environmental research, and more. An archivist is an advocate for preservation who coordinates restoration of maps, digitization of records, and creation of oral history projects.
paragraphs.zh: 幸运地拥有档案管理员的社区在整理历史记录方面具有巨大优势。有序的档案馆是人们研究祖先、进行环境研究等的场所。档案管理员是保护的倡导者，负责协调地图修复、记录数字化和口述历史项目创建。

paragraphs.en: In the words of Robert Putnam, 'People may go to the library looking mainly for information, but they find each other there.' New moms connect at baby story-times; elderly people attend events and make new friends; teenagers meet up in teen spaces after school; and readers discuss current events in the periodicals room. In libraries, community-building connections are happening all the time.
paragraphs.zh: 用罗伯特·普特南的话说：“人们去图书馆可能主要是为了寻找信息，但他们在那里找到了彼此。”新妈妈在宝宝故事时间建立联系；老年人在活动中结识新朋友；青少年放学后在青少年空间聚会；读者在期刊室讨论时事。在图书馆，社区建设的联系时刻都在发生。

paragraphs.en: As Keith Richards said, 'The public library is the great equalizer.' Despite rising costs of concert and theater tickets, public library events are often offered free of charge, enabling people of any income level to attend. Library book groups allow people to explore literary arts, and the Great Stories Club introduces at-risk youth to literature. The best part: it's all free and open to the public.
paragraphs.zh: 正如基思·理查兹所说：“公共图书馆是伟大的均衡器。”尽管音乐会和剧院门票不断上涨，公共图书馆活动通常免费提供，使任何收入水平的人都能参加。图书馆读书小组让人们探索文学艺术，“伟大故事俱乐部”则向高危青少年介绍文学。最棒的是：一切免费并向公众开放。

words.meaning: n. 均衡器，使平等的事物

paragraphs.en: In a time when education is increasingly expensive, public libraries provide information and educational opportunities free for all people, regardless of their socio-economic status. Many libraries present classes and discussion programs, and some even provide online continuing education courses. Librarians know that patrons aren't just information consumers, they're information producers.
paragraphs.zh: 在教育费用日益昂贵的时代，公共图书馆为所有人免费提供信息和教育机会，无论其社会经济地位如何。许多图书馆开设课程和讨论项目，有的甚至提供在线继续教育课程。图书馆员知道读者不仅是信息消费者，也是信息生产者。

paragraphs.en: Patrons use the library to gain knowledge in order to create their own independent works. Increasing numbers of libraries provide spaces and services for people who want to learn how to edit Wikipedia, set up blogs or podcasts, create their own magazines, and more. Many libraries offer art or writing workshops and some provide music practice rooms.
paragraphs.zh: 读者利用图书馆获取知识，以创作自己的独立作品。越来越多的图书馆为想要学习编辑维基百科、建立博客或播客、创建自己杂志等的人们提供空间和服务。许多图书馆提供艺术或写作工作坊，有的还提供音乐练习室。

paragraphs.en: The decline of civil discourse stems in part from the fact that it is so easy for people to engage only with those of similar backgrounds and ideologies. Public libraries, through programs like The Human Library and Socrates Café, can help build small communities of difference that encourage people to interact with and learn from each other through dialogue.
paragraphs.zh: 公民话语的衰落部分源于人们很容易只与背景和意识形态相似的人交往。公共图书馆通过“人类图书馆”和“苏格拉底咖啡馆”等项目，有助于建立差异化的小社区，鼓励人们通过对话相互交流和学习。

paragraphs.en: By promoting civil discourse and upholding principles of free inquiry and expression, libraries help individuals rediscover the importance of civil discourse in American life. Free tutoring, homework help, and summer reading programs for kids and teens help bridge the economic divide that impacts students' academic performance. Libraries offer homework help online, by phone, in person, and through social media.
paragraphs.zh: 通过促进公民话语并维护自由探究和表达的原则，图书馆帮助个人重新发现公民话语在美国生活中的重要性。为儿童和青少年提供的免费辅导、作业帮助和暑期阅读计划有助于弥合影响学生学业成绩的经济鸿沟。图书馆通过在线、电话、面对面和社交媒体提供作业帮助。

paragraphs.en: Annual summer reading programs also boost students' reading skills, according to a 2010 study. Through library collections, programs, and physical spaces, children learn to share, engage in their communities, participate in the arts, and explore their world. There are endless examples of innovative library services for children, including nature exploriums that engage children in learning about the natural world.
paragraphs.zh: 根据2010年的一项研究，年度暑期阅读计划也能提高学生的阅读技能。通过图书馆馆藏、项目和物理空间，孩子们学会分享、参与社区、接触艺术并探索世界。为儿童提供的创新图书馆服务不胜枚举，包括让孩子们了解自然世界的自然探索馆。

paragraphs.en: These examples are just a few of the many amazing things public libraries are doing to build strong community connections. We encourage you to try these ideas in your own libraries, and we hope they help you convince community leaders of the important role libraries play in communities large and small.
paragraphs.zh: 这些例子只是公共图书馆为建立强大社区联系所做的众多非凡之事中的一小部分。我们鼓励你在自己的图书馆尝试这些想法，并希望它们能帮助你让社区领导人信服图书馆在大小社区中扮演的重要角色。
